skill.name: n8n-code-python
skill.description: Guides writing Python for n8n Code nodes, including input access, return formats, standard-library limits, and common error patterns.
skill.summary: Build reliable Python Code node logic for n8n workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline backtick examples, and documentation links in SKILL.md. No shell execution, hidden network calls, weak cryptographic implementation, system reconnaissance, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent was found. The skill is a documentation-only guide for Python usage in n8n Code nodes.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: All external command findings are from fenced Python snippets or inline n8n helper syntax. The file documents code that users may write inside n8n, but it does not instruct the agent to run shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown code fences and examples. I found no shell invocation, subprocess usage, or command execution instruction in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation URLs Flagged as Network Risk
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the upstream source and official n8n documentation. They are references for readers and are not used by executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in metadata and an Additional Resources section. There is no code path that sends data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Base64 Reference Is Standard Library Guidance
low_findings.description: The base64 decode pattern appears in a standard-library reference section. It explains encoding and decoding operations and does not hide payloads or execute decoded data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a list of permitted Python standard-library modules. No obfuscated string, decoded command, or dynamic execution was found near the base64 example.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak algorithm usage at the flagged locations. The skill only mentions Python hashing as standard-library guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The representative flagged lines are prose, formatting examples, or checklist text. I found no weak cryptographic algorithm implementation in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts map to normal data filtering examples and safe dictionary access guidance. They do not collect host, user, process, filesystem, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines operate on n8n item JSON data or describe safe field access. No system enumeration behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Python Code nodes in n8n have strict data access and return format rules. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce workflow-safe Python snippets for transformations, validation, and aggregation.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to choose Python Beta, Python Native, JavaScript, or a built-in n8n node.
Shows how to read workflow data with _input, _json, _node, _items, and _item patterns.
Documents the required list of dictionaries return shape for Code node outputs.
Lists standard-library modules that are available in n8n Python Code nodes.
Covers common failure patterns such as webhook body nesting, missing returns, and KeyError issues.
Provides reusable patterns for filtering, aggregation, validation, string processing, and statistics.
limitations: It does not install external Python packages such as requests, pandas, numpy, or BeautifulSoup.
It does not execute n8n workflows or validate a live n8n instance.
It focuses on Python Code nodes and does not cover every n8n node type.
It recommends JavaScript for many n8n Code node tasks because n8n Python support is limited.

use_cases.title: Transform workflow item data
use_cases.description: Create Python logic that reads incoming n8n items, reshapes fields, and returns valid Code node output.

use_cases.title: Debug Python Code node errors
use_cases.description: Identify common causes of failed Code nodes, including missing return values and incorrect webhook body access.

use_cases.title: Choose the right n8n implementation path
use_cases.description: Decide whether to use Python, JavaScript, HTTP Request, Set, Filter, IF, or Switch nodes for a workflow step.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple transformation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user needs a valid Python Code node output.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Python for an n8n Code node that reads all input items, keeps the existing fields, adds a processed flag, and returns the correct structure.

prompt_templates.title: Fix webhook data access
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user has KeyError problems after a Webhook node.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my n8n Python Code node logic for webhook input. Explain the correct body access pattern and return a safer version.

prompt_templates.title: Aggregate and validate items
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced user needs batch processing in Run Once for All Items mode.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write n8n Python Code node logic that filters invalid records, calculates totals, records validation errors, and returns consistent item output.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Python versus JavaScript
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user wants an implementation recommendation before building a workflow step.
prompt_templates.prompt: Given this n8n workflow requirement, decide whether Python Code, JavaScript Code, or built-in nodes are best. Explain the tradeoffs and provide the recommended implementation approach.

output_examples.input: A workflow sends customer records that need a processed flag and normalized names.
output_examples.output: Returns a concise Python Code node approach that reads every item, preserves existing fields, normalizes name values, and emits valid n8n items.

output_examples.input: A webhook workflow fails because the code reads fields from the wrong object.
output_examples.output: Explains that webhook payload fields are under the body property and suggests safer access with defaults for missing values.

output_examples.input: A user asks whether to import requests inside a Python Code node.
output_examples.output: Recommends using an HTTP Request node before the Code node or switching to JavaScript helpers, because external Python packages are unavailable.

best_practices: Use Python only when its standard library or syntax is a clear fit for the workflow step.
Return the exact n8n item structure on every code path, including empty and error cases.
Use safe dictionary access and explicit defaults for optional workflow fields.
anti_patterns: Importing external packages that are not available inside n8n Python Code nodes.
Reading webhook fields directly from the top-level JSON object when they are under body.
Using Python Code for simple field mapping, filtering, or branching that built-in n8n nodes handle better.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps create and review Python logic for n8n Code nodes, with correct data access and return formats.

faq.question: Does it support external Python libraries?
faq.answer: No. The skill explains that n8n Python Code nodes are limited to standard-library modules.

faq.question: When should I use JavaScript instead?
faq.answer: Use JavaScript when you need n8n helper functions, HTTP helper access, stronger date handling, or broader n8n support.

faq.question: Can it fix webhook input mistakes?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains that webhook payload data is usually nested under the body property.

faq.question: Does it run or deploy n8n workflows?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and code patterns, but it does not run workflows.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
